data.title: Crude oil rises by most in two weeks as OPEC+ expected to extend output cuts
data.description:
Crude oil futures settled higher Tuesday on expectations that OPEC+ will again postpone its planned production increases when group members meet on Thursday, possibly by as much as three months.

"Given a rise in compliance with production cuts from Russia, Kazakhstan and Iraq, the lower Brent price level and indications in press reports, we assume an extension of OPEC+ production cuts until April," Goldman Sachs analysts wrote.

The cartel has been looking to gradually unwind supply cuts, but the prospect of a market surplus has pressured oil prices, with Brent trading nearly 6% below its average for December 2023.

Also contributing to oil's gains were several geopolitical factors, including a shaky ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah, the curtailed declaration of martial law in South Korea, and a rebel offensive in Syria that threatens to draw in forces from several oil producing countries.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for January closed +2.7% to $69.94/bbl, and front-month February Brent crude (CO1:COM) finished +2.5% to $73.62/bbl, the largest one-day dollar and percentage gains for both benchmarks since November 18.

U.S. natural gas futures (NG1:COM) fell sharply as warmer temperature forecasts for later this month imply less demand than had been expected; the Nymex January front-month contract settled -5.3% at $3.042/MMBtu.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

A looming global oil surplus of ~800K bbl/day should keep pressure on crude prices in 2025, Bank of America said in its 2025 energy outlook.

BofA forecasts Brent crude will average $65/bbl next year, down from ~$80 so far this year, with U.S. WTI crude at $61/bbl in 2025.

The bank sees supply by producers outside of OPEC+ rising by 1.4M bbl/day while the group seeks to unwind output cuts.

An eventual upswing in demand might be met by OPEC+ rushing in to fill the gap since the group does not want to keep losing market share, BofA head of commodity research Francisco Blanch said in presenting the outlook.

"They obviously want to bring production back, they just don't want to collapse the market in the process," he added.

data.title: Jupiter Neurosciences stock rallies 37% following $11M IPO
data.description:
• Jupiter Neurosciences (JUNS) shares closed 37% higher following the company's $11M initial public offering on Tuesday.
  • The Florida-based drug developer said it offered 2.75M shares priced at $4 per share, raising around $11M.
  • Shares of the company began trading on Tuesday under the symbol JUNS. Dominari Securities is serving as lead underwriter on the deal, with Revere Securities as co-manager, according to a statement.
  • Jupiter plans to use proceeds from the transaction in part to fund Phase 2 clinical testing of its resveratrol-based product candidate Jotrol for the treatment of Parkinson’s disease.

data.title: Silver looks cheap in 2025 even after 30% YTD rally - Commerzbank
data.description:
Front-month gold rose Tuesday for the ninth time in the past 11 sessions, as the market digests the latest U.S. economic data and its consequences for interest rate cuts.

Gold futures pared early gains after the release of the latest job openings and labor turnover survey, or JOLTS, hinted at a cautious approach to rate cuts by the Federal Reserve, while a softer dollar and easing Treasury yields - with the benchmark 10-year yield dropping to its lowest in more than a month - limited gold's pullback.

The report showed job openings in the U.S. increased in October, bucking the trend for most of the past two years and pointing to a cooling labor market.

Investor focus will turns to the ADP employment report and Fed Chair Jerome Powell's speech on Wednesday, ahead of Friday's payrolls report, which is expected to shed more light on the possibility of a December interest rate cut.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for December delivery closed +0.4% to $2,644.70/oz, while front-month December silver (XAGUSD:CUR) settled +2% to $31.077/oz; gold and silver have gained 28% and 30% YTD, respectively.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (NYSEARCA:SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

The current gold/silver ratio is trading ~85 points, roughly where it started the year, even as prices have risen 30% in 2024, which means silver is still cheap compared to gold, Commerzbank's Carsten Fritsch said.

"Like gold, silver is being supported by the interest rate cuts already implemented and the prospect of further cuts by central banks," Fritsch wrote, "because silver is also an investment metal, despite its high industrial use."

Looking ahead, Fritsch noted analysts expect another supply deficit in 2025, which will support silver's long-term price outlook.

data.title: Procaps announces financing, shareholders agreement and board updates
data.description:
• Procaps Group (NASDAQ:PROC) said on Tuesday that it has entered into a secured convertible note subscription agreement with Hoche Partners Pharma Holdings, pursuant to which the company may issue up to $40M in convertible notes.
  • Under the agreement, Procaps issued an initial $20M convertible note to Hoche. Hoche is obligated to purchase an additional $20 million of convertible notes by year-end, subject to certain exceptions.
  • The convertible notes bear an annual interest rate of 8.50%.
  • Procaps also said that under a newly established shareholder nomination agreement, it expects to welcome four new directors following the company’s upcoming Annual General Meeting on December 16, 2024.
  • Additionally, Alejandro Weinstein is expected to assume the role of Chairman of the Board.
  • Procaps has also renegotiated key debt-related agreements, resulting in the cancellation of a $5M junior unsecured subordinated note and a $2.2M reduction in accounts payable.

data.title: ATI Physical Therapy receives delisting notice from NYSE
data.description:
• ATI Physical Therapy (NYSE:ATIP) said on Tuesday that it has received notification from the New York Stock Exchange indicating that the company's Class A common stock will be delisted, and trading of its Class A common stock on the NYSE was suspended, after market close on December 3, 2024.
  • The company is currently in discussions to obtain interim financing to provide a near-term opportunity for common stockholders to obtain liquidity.
  • The company anticipates that its Class A common stock will now begin trading publicly on the OTC Pink Market.
  • This transition to the over-the-counter market is not expected to affect the company's business operations, its relationships with partners or employees, or its current Securities and Exchange Commission reporting obligations.

data.title: GXO Logistics sinks amid report it turned down acquisition offers; CEO to exit
data.description:
GXO Logistics (NYSE:GXO) dropped 10% in after hours trading amid a report that the company will remain independent after rejecting takeover offers. The company also announced that Chief Executive Officer Malcolm Wilson will retire next year.

The supply-chain services provider decided not to sell itself after exploring its options in recent months, according to a Bloomberg report on Tuesday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

GXO declined to comment to Bloomberg and didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's email request for comment.

Bloomberg first reported in early October that GXO was working with financial advisers and reviewing its options after receiving interest from potential buyers. The Oct. 10 sent GXO shares soaring 14%.

GXO announced on Tuesday that Wilson informed the board that he plans to retire next year. He will continue to lead the company during the executive search process for his successor.

"Our incoming CEO will inherit a best-in-class management team and strong industry positioning, while Malcolm will embark on a well-deserved retirement," Brad Jacobs, chairman of the GXO board said in the statement. "I fully support this decision and wish him all the best.”

data.title: Ready Capital Corporation announces pricing of public offering of senior notes due 2029
data.description:
• Ready Capital Corporation (NYSE:RC) priced an underwritten public offering of $115M aggregate principal amount of 9.00% senior notes due 2029.
  • The notes will be issued in minimum denominations and integral multiples of $25.00.
  • The company has granted to the underwriters a 30-day over-allotment option to purchase up to an additional $17.25M aggregate principal amount of the notes at the public offering price.
  • RC intends to use the net proceeds from this offering to originate or acquire target assets consistent with its investment strategy and for general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 10, 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: GXO Logistics CEO plans to retire in 2025; shares fall
data.description:
• GXO Logistics (NYSE:GXO)  announced that Malcolm Wilson, chief executive officer, plans to retire in 2025.
  • Wilson will continue to lead the company during the executive search process for the successor.
  • GXO shares slipped nearly 12% in after hours trading on Tuesday. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Iron Mountain prices debt offering
data.description:
• Iron Mountain (NYSE:IRM) priced an upsized offering by way of a private placement of $1.2B aggregate principal amount of its 6.25% Senior Notes due 2033.
  • This represents an increase of $450M in the combined aggregate principal amount of the Notes, from the previously announced amount of $750M.
  • Source: Press Release
